summary:
Claim: The only anterior tooth with mesiodistal size exceeding cervicoincisal dimension is the permanent lower central incisor.  

Evidence: - Permanent lower central incisors exhibit greater mesiodistal diameter than cervical-to-incisal height due to functional adaptation for lip support and occlusal efficiency. - Upper anterior teeth (central and lateral) are typically taller vertically, with cervicoincisal dimensions exceeding mesiodistal widths due to evolutionary adaptations for increased bite force and spatial constraints in the maxilla.  

Answer: B
